source: ACLU ALPR Tracker, NCSL, EFF, state statutes

jurisdictions.name: Alabama
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law. Law enforcement agencies operate without restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Alaska
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Arizona
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR restrictions. Phoenix and Chandler operate extensive ALPR networks.

jurisdictions.name: Arkansas
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: California
jurisdictions.notes: CA SB 34 (2016): 60-day retention limit, prohibits sharing without warrant. One of the strongest ALPR laws in the US.

jurisdictions.name: Colorado
jurisdictions.notes: HB 19-1310: 30-day retention limit for non-hit data. Requires public disclosure of ALPR usage.

jurisdictions.name: Connecticut
jurisdictions.notes: PA 12-98: 90-day retention for non-hits. Some sharing restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Delaware
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Florida
jurisdictions.notes: Some juvenile ALPR restrictions. No general retention limits for non-hit data.

jurisdictions.name: Georgia
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law. Atlanta operates extensive ALPR networks.

jurisdictions.name: Hawaii
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Idaho
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Illinois
jurisdictions.notes: Automated Traffic Law Enforcement System Act (625 ILCS 7/): 90-day retention, warrant required for data access, prohibits use for non-enforcement purposes.

jurisdictions.name: Indiana
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Iowa
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Kansas
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Kentucky
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Louisiana
jurisdictions.notes: RS 44:9.5: 60-day retention limit for some agencies. Limited restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Maine
jurisdictions.notes: LD 1561 (2015): 21-day retention limit, strict data sharing restrictions, warrant required for access.

jurisdictions.name: Maryland
jurisdictions.notes: Some local restrictions. No comprehensive statewide law.

jurisdictions.name: Massachusetts
jurisdictions.notes: State police policy limits retention to 14 days. Legislature debating broader law.

jurisdictions.name: Michigan
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law. Detroit operates large ALPR network.

jurisdictions.name: Minnesota
jurisdictions.notes: Minn. Stat. 13.824: 60-day retention for non-hit data, data sharing restrictions, audit requirements.

jurisdictions.name: Mississippi
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Missouri
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Montana
jurisdictions.notes: HB 352 (2023): Requires warrant to access ALPR data. Strong constitutional protections.

jurisdictions.name: Nebraska
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Nevada
jurisdictions.notes: NRS 481A: Some reporting requirements. No retention limits.

jurisdictions.name: New Hampshire
jurisdictions.notes: RSA 261-A: One of the most restrictive ALPR laws. 3-minute retention for non-hit plates. Cannot store non-criminal matches beyond initial processing.

jurisdictions.name: New Jersey
jurisdictions.notes: No comprehensive ALPR law. Some agency-level policies.

jurisdictions.name: New Mexico
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: New York
jurisdictions.notes: No comprehensive ALPR law. NYC operates thousands of ALPR cameras.

jurisdictions.name: North Carolina
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: North Dakota
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Ohio
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Oklahoma
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Oregon
jurisdictions.notes: ORS 802.179: Warrant required for access to ALPR data stored beyond hit. Strict sharing restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Pennsylvania
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law. Philadelphia operates extensive ALPR network.

jurisdictions.name: Rhode Island
jurisdictions.notes: 18-month retention cap (statute ambiguous). Legislative debate ongoing.

jurisdictions.name: South Carolina
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: South Dakota
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Tennessee
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Texas
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law. Texas DPS operates statewide network.

jurisdictions.name: Utah
jurisdictions.notes: UCA 41-6a-2003: 30-day retention for non-hit data. Some access restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Vermont
jurisdictions.notes: 23 V.S.A. §170: Strict warrant requirement, prohibits retention of non-hit data. Very strong privacy protections.

jurisdictions.name: Virginia
jurisdictions.notes: VA Code 15.2-2263: Some restrictions on ALPR use by localities. Pending stricter legislation.

jurisdictions.name: Washington
jurisdictions.notes: RCW 46.08.185: 30-day retention limit, audit requirements, prohibition on non-law enforcement uses.

jurisdictions.name: West Virginia
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Wisconsin
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Wyoming
jurisdictions.notes: No statewide ALPR law.

jurisdictions.name: Camden, NJ
jurisdictions.notes: Operates dense ALPR network. No local restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Chicago, IL
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to IL ALPR Act. City additionally restricts fusion center data sharing.

jurisdictions.name: New York City, NY
jurisdictions.notes: NYPD operates 4,000+ ALPR cameras. No city-level retention limits. Subject to weak state law.

jurisdictions.name: Los Angeles, CA
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to CA SB 34. LAPD must follow 60-day limit and warrant requirement.

jurisdictions.name: San Francisco, CA
jurisdictions.notes: CA SB 34 applies. SFPD has additional surveillance tech oversight ordinance (Prop B 2021).

jurisdictions.name: Boston, MA
jurisdictions.notes: BPD policy: 14-day retention. City council considering stronger ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Seattle, WA
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to WA state 30-day limit. Seattle IT has additional surveillance transparency ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Portland, OR
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to OR warrant requirement. City also has surveillance technology transparency policy.

jurisdictions.name: Denver, CO
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to CO HB 19-1310 (30-day limit). DPD must publish annual ALPR usage reports.

jurisdictions.name: Minneapolis, MN
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to MN state 60-day limit. Post-2020 reforms added additional MPD data restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Detroit, MI
jurisdictions.notes: DPD operates large ALPR network with Project Green Light. No city-level restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Phoenix, AZ
jurisdictions.notes: Large ALPR deployment. No city or state restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Houston, TX
jurisdictions.notes: HPD operates extensive ALPR network. No restrictions at city or state level.

jurisdictions.name: Atlanta, GA
jurisdictions.notes: APD operates ALPR with no retention limits or state law restricting use.

jurisdictions.name: Philadelphia, PA
jurisdictions.notes: PPD uses Shotspotter + ALPR. No city or state restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Miami, FL
jurisdictions.notes: MPD operates ALPR citywide. No meaningful restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Dallas, TX
jurisdictions.notes: DPD uses ALPR extensively. No state or local restrictions.

jurisdictions.name: Las Vegas, NV
jurisdictions.notes: LVMPD uses ALPR at high density due to gaming/casino district monitoring.

jurisdictions.name: Richmond, VA
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to VA state law. RPD has local policy limiting non-investigative ALPR queries.

jurisdictions.name: Salt Lake City, UT
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to UT 30-day retention law. SLCPD publishes annual ALPR usage statistics.

jurisdictions.name: Nashville, TN
jurisdictions.notes: MNPD uses ALPR citywide. No restrictions at city or state level.

jurisdictions.name: Portland, ME
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to Maine LD 1561. 21-day limit, warrant required.

jurisdictions.name: Manchester, NH
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to NH RSA 261-A. 3-minute retention for non-hit plates.

jurisdictions.name: Concord, CA
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to CA SB 34. CPD participates in regional ALPR network under state law.

jurisdictions.name: Alameda County, CA
jurisdictions.notes: ACSO subject to CA SB 34. County also has surveillance ordinance requiring board approval for new tech.

jurisdictions.name: Contra Costa County, CA
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to CA SB 34.

jurisdictions.name: Cook County, IL
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to IL ALPR Act. County operates extensive network covering Chicago suburbs.

jurisdictions.name: King County, WA
jurisdictions.notes: Subject to WA state 30-day limit. KCSO has additional transparency policies.
